notes: All 5 Phase-4 tasks (mxdx-awe.17, .18, .19, .20, .50) executed and closed on branch brains/rust-p2p-interactive. Re-architecture question (2026-04-16) resolved by user Option C — keep mxdx_session_key canonical, migrate npm to match, coordinated release is now the project default (new ADR 2026-04-16-coordinated-rust-npm-releases.md, addendum to 2026-04-15-mcall-wire-format.md). T-40 + T-44 shipped as paired coordinated-release commits per the new policy. T-40 (mxdx-awe.17, commit feaa25c): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/signaling/events.rs — CallInvite/CallAnswer/CallCandidates/CallHangup/CallSelectAnswer serde structs with Matrix VoIP wire shape (call_id, party_id, version: "1", lifetime, offer/answer SDP, candidates, reason, selected_party_id). mxdx_session_key field on CallInvite (Option<String>, skip_serializing_if = None) carries base64 AES-256 key; build_invite(call_id, party_id, sdp, sealed_key, lifetime, session_uuid) is the ONLY public path that embeds a SealedKey — SealedKey::to_invite_b64 (new pub(crate) accessor in crypto.rs) wraps the existing pub(in crate::crypto) as_bytes without widening visibility. SealedKey::from_invite_b64 accessor for Phase 5's answerer path. CallInvite has hand-rolled Debug that redacts mxdx_session_key as <redacted>. ICE candidate shape uses Matrix spec camelCase sdpMid/sdpMLineIndex (both Option, both skip_serializing_if = None) — matches npm node emitter (sdpMid only) and npm browser emitter (both). DEFAULT_INVITE_LIFETIME_MS = 30_000 matches npm post-T-44. Committed cross-runtime fixture at tests/fixtures/signaling-vectors.json with 9 vectors. tests/signaling_vectors.rs: 10 byte-exact emit assertions + parse roundtrip (8 passed + 1 ignored regen). 9 unit tests in events.rs module. T-44 (mxdx-awe.50, commit 2abc291): coordinated npm migration per ADR 2026-04-16-coordinated-rust-npm-releases.md. packages/core/p2p-signaling.js: sendInvite emits content.mxdx_session_key (was session_key); default lifetime 30000 (was 60000). packages/launcher/src/runtime.js:1621-1622 and packages/web-console/src/terminal-view.js:383-384: answerer reads mxdx_session_key. packages/web-console/dist/: Vite rebuild (old tracked index-Chsrz6sy.js + mxdx_core_wasm_bg-DEuP2-Bl.wasm deleted; replaced by rebuilt index-C1mWJaDZ.js). New cross-runtime sidecar test packages/e2e-tests/tests/rust-npm-signaling-vectors.test.js: 16 tests in 3 passes — PARSE (npm JSON.parse reads Rust-emitted fixture into expected fields), EMIT (P2PSignaling.sendInvite/sendAnswer/sendCandidates/sendHangup/sendSelectAnswer emits byte-for-byte the Rust fixture for identical logical inputs), REGRESSION GUARDS (default lifetime 30000, legacy session_key field name never emitted). All 16 passed. Existing p2p-signaling.test.js: 5 passed (no regression). T-41 (mxdx-awe.18, commit d99e7d1): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/signaling/parse.rs exposes parse_event(&str), parse_value(&Value), parse_content(event_type, &Value). ParsedCallEvent enum with Invite/Answer/Candidates/Hangup/SelectAnswer + Unknown{event_type} fallthrough for forward-compat with future Matrix VoIP spec additions (m.call.reject, m.call.negotiate). ParseError::{InvalidJson, NotACallEvent, InvalidContent} — never panics on arbitrary bytes. Exports CALL_EVENT_TYPES constant. 14 unit tests cover every variant + error paths + fuzz-style binary inputs. tests/signaling_parse.rs: 8 golden integration tests loading the committed fixture through parse_event (all variants). tests/signaling_parser_fuzz.rs: compile-only fuzz stub with fuzz_one(&[u8]) signature matching libfuzzer-sys convention; #[ignore]d so it doesn't run in default CI but compiles (T-41 acceptance satisfied). T-42 (mxdx-awe.19, commit beb61f6): crates/mxdx-p2p/src/signaling/glare.rs — pure function resolve(our_user_id: &str, their_user_id: &str, our_call_id: &str, their_call_id: &str) -> GlareResult {WeWin, TheyWin}. Uses &str (not ruma UserId) to avoid pulling matrix-sdk into mxdx-p2p's wasm-compat surface. Lower lexicographic user_id wins; tie-break on call_id (lower wins); degenerate a==b && ca==cb returns WeWin deterministically with prop_assume!-excluded agreement property. GlareResult::invert() is public (also used by Phase 5 state machine reasoning). Side-channel analysis: compares only public values, match arms return constants, no secret-dependent timing. proptest 1.x as dev-dep only (pure Rust, wasm-compatible). 4 proptest properties (agreement_both_peers_converge, total_no_panic_on_arbitrary_strings, deterministic, agreement_holds_when_user_ids_tie) + 3 concrete spec cases + 4 in-module unit tests. T-43 (mxdx-awe.20, commit b6236e0): crates/mxdx-matrix/src/client.rs adds classify_sync_event pure function extracted from the existing inline match in sync_and_collect_events. Refactored original inline match arms at two sites (lines ~910 and ~960) to call the classifier — same runtime behavior, now unit-testable. All m.call.* types (including unknown ones for forward-compat) classify as Deliver. crates/mxdx-matrix/src/lib.rs exports CALL_EVENT_TYPES constant mirroring mxdx-p2p's (kept duplicated to avoid a mxdx-matrix → mxdx-p2p dep which would invert the direction). MatrixClient::send_call_event(room_id, event_type, content) thin wrapper over room.send_raw: validates event_type starts with m.call., refuses non-E2EE rooms (cardinal rule), routes through the same Megolm-in-flight path as send_event / send_megolm. No new encryption code. 8 new unit tests for the classifier + CALL_EVENT_TYPES lock-list. Pre-existing flaky integration test tombstone_room_marks_room_replaced (tuwunel-dependent) fails identically before and after — filtered from assertions per teammate brief. Final nurture+secure touch-up (commit 913432e): #[allow(dead_code)] on SealedKey::from_invite_b64 with rationale (wasm target warning; Phase 5 wires the caller); glare module docstring gains side-channel analysis paragraph.

verification_results.cargo_test_mxdx_p2p: 111 passed, 3 ignored (up from 78 post-T-40; 19 new from T-40 + 22 new from T-41 + 14 new from T-42)
verification_results.cargo_check_wasm: 0 warnings, 0 errors — no matrix-sdk pulled into wasm dep graph
verification_results.security_grep_gate: green — no forbidden patterns in crates/mxdx-p2p

key_decisions: Byte-exact wire compatibility with packages/core/p2p-signaling.js locked via committed JSON fixture + sidecar test asserting byte-equality on JSON.stringify output — not just 'looks similar'.

SealedKey sealing preserved: to_invite_b64 / from_invite_b64 are pub(crate) in crypto.rs; only build_invite in the sibling signaling::events module uses them; raw SealedKey bytes remain pub(in crate::crypto).
CallInvite uses hand-rolled Debug that redacts mxdx_session_key — locked by invite_debug_redacts_mxdx_session_key test.
Glare resolver uses &str (not ruma UserId) — wasm-compat preserved (mxdx-p2p stays lean, no matrix-sdk in its wasm dep graph). npm reference also uses plain strings.

Degenerate identical-input case in glare returns WeWin deterministically; agreement invariant explicitly excludes this case via prop_assume!. Phase 5 short-circuits before calling resolve with identical inputs.
CALL_EVENT_TYPES duplicated between mxdx-p2p::signaling::parse and mxdx-matrix lib root — documented cross-reference, avoided cycle/inverted-dep. Both reference the 2026-04-15 wire-format ADR.

mxdx-matrix sync filter is NOT a positive allowlist — extracted to classify_sync_event pure function with a small denylist. All m.call.* types pass, including unknown ones for forward-compat with future Matrix spec additions.
send_call_event refuses non-E2EE rooms (cardinal rule) and routes through the same room.send_raw path as send_event/send_megolm — zero new encryption code.
